Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 02 - Su.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 02 - Super Mario (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 02 - Su.notes: With over 100 tracks, Super Mario is of course very well represented in smash. So much so that it's almost overflowing with shit main themes from spin-off titles like Mario & Luigi and Mario Tennis. But if you can look past them, and the 15+ Main Theme remixes, you can find some banger remixes.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 03 - Ma.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 03 - Mario Kart (Wii, 3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2008).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 03 - Ma.notes: Mario Kart music truly is the best and although I'm delighted by the slim pickings that are here, they could have done so many banger remixes of some old tracks!! Instead they have a bunch of the worst songs from Mario Kart 8. I wish they had done more than literally one song from Super, 64, Super Circuit, Double Dash, DS, Wii...

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Do.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Do.notes: I love Donkey Kong music and although I would have loved for more, I feel like some of these remixes are so fucking good. Over smash history they have included an outrageous 8 versions of Jungle Level, but when they have diversified, they've smashed it. Stickerbush Symphony, Crocodile Cacophony and Map/Bonus Level are all so fucking good.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 05 - Th.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 05 - The Legend of Zelda (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 05 - Th.notes: Zelda, arguably one of the best Nintendo franchises soundtrack-wise, is not very well represented in the smash remixes front. There's a lot of generic this and that. Although that's not to say there aren't diamonds in the rough. The Molgera remix is one of my favourites in the whole game!

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 06 - Me.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 06 - Metroid (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 06 - Me.notes: I really feel like Metroid is a tough cookie to work with, but Smash really knocked it out of the park, especially with the early remixes. Those remixes were so good that they put the NES Metroid's soundtrack on the map. The selection of original tracks towards the end are disappointing and they're missing some potential big hits.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 07 - Yo.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 07 - Yoshi (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 07 - Yo.notes: Sadly, Yoshi's Story became a legal stage in Ultimate, meaning everyone has played these songs to death. I get it, but I love Yoshi music and they will forever remain in my heart, even if I've heard them too much. Yoshi's Story was a big game for me as a child and I just love it so there. I can't forgive the child-like off-key whistling in many of these songs though.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 08 - Ki.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 08 - Kirby (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 08 - Ki.notes: There are some legendary Kirby remixes in smash. It must be because both Kirby and Smash are Sakurai's babies. I love Kirby's unique, extreme and happy-go-lucky style of music, often very maximalist and fast-paced. Every now and then a huge banger boss theme.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 09 - St.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 09 - Star Fox (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 09 - St.notes: The Star Fox Assault songs alone make this worth it, but it is a rare case where the smash remixes don't really do it for me. There's an element of having heard these tracks a little too much given all the years that Lylat Cruise was legal. A shame we got no new remixes in Ultimate.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Po.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Po.notes: Pokemon of course has excellent music and there are many tracks from its rich history represented here with excellent remixes (especially from Melee), but the truth is, since playing on Pokemon Stadium 2 so often as a competitive smash player, I've overheard all of these songs and can no longer truly appreciate them.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 11 - F-.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 11 - F-Zero (Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2001).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 11 - F-.notes: F-Zero music in smash is excellent! Some of the very best remixes are here, and the original tracks are largely also great. In Ultimate they added all the tracks from the SNES F-Zero which is a bit much, and also the horrible F-Zero medley but apart from that it's 10s across the board.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 12 - Ea.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 12 - EarthBound (Mother) (Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2001).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 12 - Ea.notes: I love the Mother series so much, and the amount of love that Smash has shown it really is unparalleled. Shoutouts to Sakurai, man. Every single one of these tracks is a remix, although I happen to know that some of them were altered slightly from already existing remix albums of the series. Anyway this music is different from other franchises for its heart and soul. It's quirky, and has lots of emotion.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 13 - Fi.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 13 - Fire Emblem (Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2001).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 13 - Fi.notes: They really did an excellent job with Fire Emblem. It's a very long series and they honour each game! Well, apart from Fates. So much Fates music didn't make it in, which breaks my heart because it's my favourite Fire Emblem. Still the early and Japan-only tracks are well represented and the quality of remixes is very high.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 14 - Ga.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 14 - Game & Watch (Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2001).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 14 - Ga.notes: It's always pissed me off that Game & Watch gets its own franchise section in the game when games like Ice Climber and Duck Hunt don't. Why not just put these tracks in the other section like all the others? There's just two of them.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 15 - Ki.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 15 - Kid Icarus (Wii, 3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2008).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 15 - Ki.notes: I feel like the original two remixes for Overworld and Underworld are the highlights. The remixes from Uprising just do not hit for me at all. I was never crazy for Uprising. But it's still a shame that Kid Icarus got absolutely nothing in Ultimate.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - Wa.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - WarioWare (3DS, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2008).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - Wa.notes: Tragically, Wario games have been treated terribly when it comes to smash. The entire rich history of Wario Land has been erased, despite them adding a costume for Wario and changing his victory theme. There is so much lost potential there, it hurts. WarioWare itself is represented OK, but there was nothing new in Ultimate which makes me sad. At least the strength of the original Brawl remixes carries us on. But my heart breaks for Wario Land.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 17 - Pi.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 17 - Pikmin (Wii, 3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2008).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 17 - Pi.notes: Pikmin music is something that I feel very strongly about. It's got a very special, wacky, extraterrestrial vibe, which is reflected in its wheep-whoop instrumentation and funky harmonies. It's also above all a peaceful game with peaceful music. I can imagine it's very hard to translate that into engaging battle music, but at least a couple of the remixes are solid. Otherwise, I have to say the spirit of Pikmin isn't as gorgeously represented in Smash as I would have liked. I could have done with more, and embracing the weird a bit more.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 18 - An.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 18 - Animal Crossing (Wii, 3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2008).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 18 - An.notes: I love Animal Crossing with all my heart and I think these remixes are excellent, but sitting down and listening to them for this project hurt because I've heard some of these literally thousands of times over the years. There should be studies on how all these competitive smash players are being exposed to Animal Crossing music so much. Imagine if it was the Game and Watch stage instead!

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 19 - Wi.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 19 - Wii Fit (3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2014).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 19 - Wi.notes: You'd never think this would happen but they really went above and beyond for Wii Fit. I wouldn't have known how much the original soundtrack slaps if it weren't for some of these excellent remixes. I got so excited by how good they are, I put them together in a small mix which can you download on this very website! The arrangers really did a great job with capturing the Nintendo Wii vibe and making them fast-paced for battle. Same goes for Wii Sports.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 20 - Pu.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 20 - Punch-Out!! (3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2014).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 20 - Pu.notes: Smash Ultimate really did Punch Out dirty by not putting in any new music. These measly 5 tracks all sound the same and are super plain. I'm not saying that Punch Out is a wealth of great source material but they could have made more of an effort. Maybe even put in some original NES music, this is all from the Wii game.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 21 - Xe.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 21 - Xenoblade Chronicles (3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2014).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 21 - Xe.notes: Xenoblade music is overrated in my opinion. It all blends into each other. It goes for this epic orchestral style which just isn't for me. I get that it's done by the composers who did a bunch of remixes for smash that I love, but something about the vibe of the game doesn't do it for me. That being said, there are some classics in there. Gaur Plain is great.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 22 - Sp.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 22 - Splatoon (Switch) (gamerip) (2018).json

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 23 - Me.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 23 - Metal Gear (Switch, Wii) (gamerip) (2008).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 23 - Me.notes: Crazy good how some of these remixes are. There isn't much to chew on here but what is there is gold. I don't even like Metal Gear but I can't deny the intense spy music is quality.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 24 - So.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 24 - Sonic (Wii, 3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2008).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 24 - So.notes: 2D sonic music? Stellar. 10/10. Would recommend to a friend. Literally anything from 3D Sonic? Stay away from me. No. It's hard to overstate how bad these rock songs are. But simply don't listen to them: Smash at least included some absolute bangers from the classic games. Also, devastating that there's only ever been one remix...

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 25 - Me.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 25 - Mega Man (3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2014).json

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 26 - PA.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 26 - PAC-MAN (3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2014).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 26 - PA.notes: For shitty arcade music, there is a surprisingly good selection of remixes here! I hadn't truly appreciated how great they were until more recently. There are also a bunch of medleys of old games but I don't think they're very nice to listen to.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 27 - St.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 27 - Street Fighter (3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2015).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 27 - St.notes: They exclusively have music from Street Fighter 2 in here, OK sure, I don't know enough about the series to demand more, although I know that 3rd Strike has some bangers that we're missing out on. It's fine because Yoko's soundtrack for this game is genuinely great. Funny thing is, the whole ass soundtrack is here! Not only that, but both the original and the re-release's upgraded music as well. All 12 characters are represented, two times. Honestly I'm down for this approach and they could have done it with other games too. The few remixes which are there are also fab.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 28 - Fi.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 28 - Final Fantasy (3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2015).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 28 - Fi.notes: One of Smash 4's tragedies was only including two tracks from Final Fantasy on the Midgar stage, but thankfully, the Sephiroth DLC fixed that with some choice new picks, even though getting the rights to the music is clearly very tough (it was hard to download these tracks for this website!). I like that just have some classic, epic, final fantasy music now.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 29 - Ba.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 29 - Bayonetta (3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2016).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 29 - Ba.notes: Bayonetta music is something special. It sounds like nothing else. It really captures the sexy over the top vibes of the game perfectly and I'm honestly so impressed. In smash they have a good selection of original tracks, and two remixes which only really swap out vocals because I guess they couldn't get the rights to the singer. It's fine, this music really doesn't need much remixing, they already carry the full weight of Bayonetta's drama and class with them. I'm obsessed. And I'm pissed that they added literally nothing new in Ultimate!

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 30 - Ca.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 30 - Castlevania (Switch) (gamerip) (2018).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 30 - Ca.notes: Castlevania is great because is always has classical music inspired rock music with a gothic flair, which translates beautifully to smash remixes, of which there are many. That being said, there's also a lot of remixes from Judgment and Harmony of Despair which are all pretty mid. Would have been better with more original versions of the songs!

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 32 - Pe.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 32 - Persona (Switch) (gamerip) (2019).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 32 - Pe.notes: Persona 5 really has some legendary music and the best songs are here! The other persona games though, I'm not convinced by them at all. They're sounding too anime for my tastes.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 33 - Dr.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 33 - Dragon Quest (Switch) (gamerip) (2019).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 33 - Dr.notes: Dragon Quest music was only ever composed by this one dude who made classical music with an orchestra first, and then translated it to chiptunes. Honestly, I respect that, and it means that you play a remake of any Dragon Quest game today and you're straight up listening to classical music as you play, which is in itself a whole vibe. For smash though, the guy let them use one adventure and one battle theme from each game and said that'll do. They all kinda sound the same, there are no fun remixes, and anyone who has played the games will be immediately bored of them because there is so little variation in the games.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 34 - Ba.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 34 - Banjo-Kazooie (Switch, Switch 2) (gamerip) (2019).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 34 - Ba.notes: I enjoy that Banjo Kazooie is here because Grant Kirkhope has just such a ridiculous style of music and it's amazing to see it get remixed like this! It really reminds me of my childhood. I think the remixes are all great. Love live Banjo Kazooie!

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 35 - Fa.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 35 - Fatal Fury (Switch) (gamerip) (2019).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 35 - Fa.notes: They really brought in like 50 tracks from throughout SNK history, pretty cool. Old fighting games have great music so this is awesome. It's a shame Street Fighter couldn't do the same. There are some great tracks in here, although I would say Tekken's selection is overall way better.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 36 - AR.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 36 - ARMS (Switch) (gamerip) (2020).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 36 - AR.notes: The ARMS soundtrack slaps and they just went ahead and put the whole thing in. That main theme is just so good. The voices singing oooOOOooooOOOooo are funny and feature in most of the songs. I'll always have a soft spot for ARMS, even if Min Min in Smash is a war criminal.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 37 - Mi.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 37 - Minecraft (Switch) (gamerip) (2020).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 37 - Mi.notes: Minecraft was always going to be a struggle. It's mostly ambient music so how on earth are you meant to make battle remixes for smash? Sakurai attempts to solve this problem by hiring his very best remixers to tackle obscure-ass tracks from Minecraft Dungeons and the minigames from the discontinued console editions of the Minecraft. There's no actual music from Minecraft. I really think they could have gone for Stal or Sweden or something for jokes, or Lena Raine. I think there was potential there but this selection is shit because the source material is so weak.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 38 - Te.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 38 - Tekken (Switch) (gamerip) (2021).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 38 - Te.notes: I fucking hate Kazuya but I can't deny that him joining smash was great for this wealth of excellent Tekken music that came with him. Really good, especially the early Tekkens. Tekken 6 and 7 kinda era is not really my vibe, but the first like 20 tracks here are fucking great. So good to have actual dance music in smash!

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 39 - Ki.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 39 - Kingdom Hearts (Switch) (gamerip) (2021).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 39 - Ki.notes: Listen, I love Yoko Shimomura, but these tracks are just not it. In my humble opinion she really missed with this soundtrack and now we have to deal with that because Hollow Bastion is legal. I've heard these tracks to death and I'm sick and tired of them! I can't rate them in good conscience because of my resentment.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 31 - Ot.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 31 - Other (3DS, GC, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2001).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 31 - Ot.notes: The 'Other' section is a vast and wild, scary place. There are over a 100 tracks from obscure-ass games in here. Some retro characters were relegated here too. You really can get anything in here, but I have to say there are some gems hidden in amongst the trash. All of Pilotwings is right there! This section is definitely not to be overlooked.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 01 - Su.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 01 - Super Smash Bros. (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 01 - Su.notes: Wow I totally underestimated just how many original smash tracks there are. It took me so long to listen to all these, and many of them sound the same. That being said, shout-outs to smash for having the most iconic main themes ever. All 5 of them knock it out of the park honestly. I removed a lot of the jingles and stuff.
